name: Spyro
appearances: Gill Grunt and the Curse of the Fish Master
Stump Smash Crosses the Bone Dragon
Trigger Happy Targets the Evil Kaos
description: Spyro is a purple dragon who is the titular character of Skylanders: Spyro's Adventure, and a Core Skylander in the franchise. He is one of the three Skylanders that come with the console version starter pack of Spyro's Adventure, the other two being Gill Grunt and Trigger Happy. Additionally, he has Dark, Legendary, and Eon's Elite counterparts. His Series 3 counterpart is called Mega Ram Spyro.
biography: Spyro hails from a rare line of magical purple dragons that come from a faraway land few have ever traveled. It's been said that the Scrolls of the Ancients mention Spyro prominently -- the old Portal Masters having chronicled his many exciting adventures and heroic deeds. Finally, it was Master Eon himself who reached out and invited him to join the Skylanders. Now calling Skylands his home, Spyro remains one of its most valued protectors, with evil facing a new enemy -- and the Skylanders gaining a valued ally.
personality: Although eager and young at heart, Spyro is a strong-willed dragon who is fearless and brave; a born hero dedicated to defending his world from all things evil. He holds a photographic memory, and as a result, has encyclopedic knowledge of all the islands and creatures in Skylands he has encountered, more so than any other Skylander. He is always up for adventure (his favorite kind being treasure hunts), and is the first Skylander to leap into action. Spyro is a usual quick learner, able to master new abilities in a matter of hours. Due to this, he is convinced that he can be good at everything so he can put his new powers to good use to help defend Skylands. However Spyro isn't used to not being able to learn new abilities right away, feeling greatly disheartened when he isn't able to harness them despite being trained by Master Eon, the greatest among the Portal Masters. Although he is well in the company of his companions, Spyro possesses a headstrong and impulsive side. In The Machine of Doom, he believed that he could do everything by himself, implying that Spyro was used to doing heroics on his own prior to becoming a Skylander. This resulted in him struggling to work in a team at first (with his Skylander companions always reminding Spyro that he doesn't have to do everything by himself). However, his headstrong trait has presumably diminished during the course of being a Skylander when Spyro gradually became more humble, as shown in Spyro versus The Mega Monsters when he shared his victory over Kaos with the other Skylanders who helped him. He always keeps his temper in check, but there are times when he allows his emotions to get the better of him. After many adventures with the Skylanders, Spyro grew into a natural leader dedicated to scorching the forces of darkness. He takes his role as a Skylander seriously after being taught by Master Eon and holds deep insight regarding his fellow Skylanders, seeing them as more than just a team, but family, making them an unstoppable force against the toughest of foes. According to Roller Brawl, Spyro's leadership is on par with a roller derby's pivot, 'establishing the team's strategy and sets the pace of the pack'. In Skylanders: Imaginators, Spyro still displays a slight cocky behavior and occasionally cracks a joke or two, much to the dismay of certain Skylanders. According to him, he is awesome, but a Skylander who does pretty good in handling Doomlanders is pretty cool in his book.

story.content: While looking for new ideas for the Spyro the Dragon license in 2007, Activision and Toys for Bob explored many concepts with Spyro, including a 'realistic, gritty Spyro', a 'tiny Spyro', and an 'Origami Spyro'. Eventually the toys-to-life idea of Spyro's Kingdom came into light. Spyro was going to be a full-grown dragon and the king of Spyro's Kingdom, but it was felt they couldn't have a game called Spyro's Kingdom without Spyro as a playable character, so Fire Dragon became Spyro.

Spyro is the great, stout-hearted hero of legend, hailing from a rare race of magic purple dragons. From a young age, Spyro became a warrior against evil and protector of the peace. Master Eon found Spyro playing in the Summer Forest and asked for his help in protecting Skylands. During his time as a Skylander, Spyro defeated the evil dragon Cynder, freeing her from the sinister control of Malefor, and later convinced Eon to sign her up as a Skylander. He also learned to control focused dark magic to become Dark Spyro.

Spyro, Gill Grunt, Eruptor and Boomer were on Eon's island when Hugo informed them of flying sheep attacking the Mabu Market. They discovered the sheep were being carried by invisible trolls led by a Spell Punk. The Skylanders journeyed to the Forest of Fear to find the Chattering Key. Despite facing hallucinations and the stone Wyvern Fido, they retrieved the key, only for Kaos to steal it to unlock the Machine of Doom. Spyro ultimately saved Kaos from being buried alive in the crumbling pyramid, remembering the lessons Master Eon taught him.

While training his Daybringer Flame attack, Spyro was tasked with finding a monster in Stinky Swamp. After encountering Kaos, who claimed to have turned over a new leaf, Spyro remained suspicious. When Kaos attempted to blackmail the Eternal Archives curator, Stealth Elf used Kaos's magic staff to enlarge Spyro to giant size, allowing him to defeat Kaos's mega-sized Chompies. Spyro realized Kaos's true plan was to attack the Archive and steal the Book of Power.

After Malefor returned and stole the powers of the Skylanders, Spyro, Cynder, Hex, and Wallop pursued the villain to the Skylanders Academy. Despite being weakened, Spyro challenged Malefor. With the help of Calliope and Persephone, Spyro destroyed Malefor's chestplate and the crystal inside, releasing the stolen powers. The Skylanders then used full strength to tie Malefor's tail to a ship's anchor, dragging him away from the Academy.

Spyro and Stealth Elf were engaged in a friendly race when a Doomlander emerged and stole a book of Imaginite. Spyro tracked the creature to the Cradle of Creation and met with the new Portal Master and their Imaginator. When Kaos and the Brain used Mind Magic to control all of Skylands, Spyro was immune because he is a dragon. He journeyed to the Dragon Temple and used dragon magic to undo the brainwashing spell across Skylands.

content: I'd like to see you try, Kaos!
Mess with the dragon, get the horns!
I knew I could do it!
Uh, are my horns sticking out?
You told us not to underestimate him. I won't do it again.
Just trying to save your life, that's all.
Kaos is as evil as they come. This is a man who would give candy to a baby just so he could steal it back again.
It's time you three picked on someone your own size!
It was a team effort, Master. It's always a team effort.
We're Skylanders! We're never helpless and we never give up!
The biggest part of succeeding anything... is believing in yourself.
Let's see how you like surprises, Frightbeard! Skylanders! Clear the decks!
Look, I know some Skylanders have given you a hard time in the past, but I've never doubted your loyalties.
His name is Malefor, the self-crowned Undead Dragon King of Skylands.
Malefor might be powerful, Cynder, but we're Skylanders. That makes us more than a team, that makes us family. And that makes us unstoppable!
Zook's gotta get through that gate.
What do you say we teach Big Head a little lesson?

trivia: Throughout the games, Spyro's design features differ in gameplay and media, such as his horns, spines, and claw color.
In the Ruins of Skylanders: Spyro's Adventure, the young Mabu Quigley will say 'Haven't I seen you somewhere before?' if you use Spyro to talk to him.
According to the novel The Machine of Doom, Spyro was first found by Master Eon in Summer Forest, a homeworld from Spyro 2: Ripto's Rage!.
Spyro has the most variants of any Skylander, including Series 1, Dark, Legendary, Series 2, Series 3, Series 3 Dark, Mini, and Eon's Elite.
He is the only Console Starter Pack Skylander in Spyro's Adventure not to wield a weapon.
Spyro was one of the original four Skylanders made for the game's first prototype, Spyro's Kingdom.
In Imaginators, he is voiced by Matthew Mercer, taking over from Josh Keaton.

trailers.name: Meet the Skylanders- Spyro l Skylanders Spyro's Adventure l Skylanders
Skylanders Giants - Meet the Skylanders - Series 2 Spyro (All Fired Up)
Skylanders Swap Force - Meet the Skylanders - Mega Ram Spyro (All Fired Up)

gameplay: As a magical purple dragon, Spyro has the ability to harness the power of the other elements, though he prefers to master fire. He can also charge at his enemies with his horns and knock down obstacles, even lighting them ablaze with the element of fire. Being a dragon, Spyro has the ability to fly. When energies of darkness are nearby, Spyro can use their power to transform into his dark alter ego - Dark Spyro. In Skylanders: Battlecast, Spyro's abilities mostly stay the same, though with debuffing effects to some of them; however, he also gains the ability to brand enemies magically with the Sigil of the Dragon. His Special Ability is Dragon Breath, which attacks all enemies, and his signature Gear is the Ampli-fier, steel enhancements with engraved runes for his wings that power him and his allies up.

basic.name: Dragon's Fire
basic.content: Spyro breathes balls of flame from his mouth, incinerating enemies.

basic.name: Charge
basic.content: Spyro runs head down knocking over enemies with his horns.

basic_upgrades.name: Long Range Raze
basic_upgrades.content: Enhancing Spyro's lung capacity, this ability lets Spyro shoot Flameballs that travel much farther than before.

basic_upgrades.name: Spyro's Flight
basic_upgrades.content: Spyro takes to the skies. Well, off the ground at least! Fly over water and move at great speed.

basic_upgrades.name: Sprint Charge
basic_upgrades.content: Fitness counts. Spyro is able to charge for a longer distance with this upgrade.

soulgems.name: Spyro's Earth Pound
soulgems.content: Perform a devastating dive-bomb head smash while flying, damaging enemies all around you.

wow_pows.name: Fireslam!
wow_pows.content: While flying, press Attack 2 to allow Spyro to shoot fireballs and perform a flaming slam.

wow_pows.name: Head Start!
wow_pows.content: Summon a magic worm ally at the end of your charge and send it after your enemies.

abilities.name: Fire Shield
abilities.content: This upgrade generates fire shields around Spyro when he uses his Flameball attack.

abilities.name: Exploding Fireblast
abilities.content: Spyro's central Flameball ignites into an explosive force that not only does more damage but hits multiple enemies.

abilities.name: The Daybringer Flame
abilities.content: Hold down your primary power button to build up this attack, which generates a flame so big and bright it is said to bring daylight to the sky.

abilities.name: Stun Charge
abilities.content: Enemies hit by Spyro's charge become temporarily stunned.

abilities.name: Comet Dash
abilities.content: A magic aura surrounds Spyro's horns imbuing them with extra power and damage that burns enemies in its path.

abilities.name: Ibex's Wrath Charge
abilities.content: Find Spyro's extra gear. With this upgrade, Spyro builds up speed and such a destructive force that it causes the very air around him to ignite into the magical form of fiery ibex horns.
